meaning: n.底,底部,尽头,末端adj.底部的vt.装底,查明真相,测量深浅vi.到达底部,建立基础
examples: He sat at the bottom of the stairs.
She says $95,000 is her bottom line.
The marriage had reached rock bottom.
This creates a bottom for the market by putting a price on the most problematic paper.
The result means Hamilton remain bottom of the Scottish Premier League, two points behind Falkirk.
